diffSummary: Render the account's own messages sent from another Zalo device (the original Zalo app on phone/web). Root cause: the production session-start path command/login_and_listen logged in via zalo::login(credentials), which calls zalo::login_with(.., self_listen=false). With self_listen off, the zca-rust realtime listener drops every event where message.is_self (confirmed in the pinned rev c95c88b, src/listen.rs: `if message.is_self && !self_listen { continue; }` for both user cmd 501 and group cmd 521), so zca-desktop only ever saw other people's incoming messages and never the user's own outbound messages from other devices. Fix (one logical change, command layer): login_and_listen now establishes the session with zalo::login_with(credentials, true) so the listener surfaces self-authored messages. All four production session-start commands (start_listening, start_qr_login, restore_sessions, start_listening_from_file) funnel through login_and_listen, so the single flip covers every listener path. The whole downstream pipeline already carries is_self/isSelf: to_incoming_message copies it, the bridge emits the unchanged non-secret IncomingMessage DTO with no direction filtering, persist_incoming uses bump_unread=!is_self, and the frontend renders outgoing: msg.isSelf. The same-device send echo (which self_listen now also redelivers) is reconciled by the pre-existing dedupe from fix-outgoing-echo-dupe on both seams (store save_message: same account+thread+body, different msg_id, within 15s; frontend appendToBucket: merge the echo into the optimistic just-sent bubble), so enabling self_listen does not double-render this app's own sends. No new layer, no new public API (login_with already existed and is used by live tests), no credential/IPC-surface change.

checks.name: build
checks.summary: cargo build compiles with login_and_listen using zalo::login_with(credentials, true).

checks.name: tests
checks.summary: cargo test offline suite passes (35 passed; 0 failed; 13 ignored). The store outgoing-echo dedupe and per-account routing tests stay green with the self_listen-enabled production listen path.

checks.name: lint
checks.summary: cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean with the login_and_listen change.

checks.name: smoke
checks.summary: Live, owner-verified on the running app (bun run tauri dev): a message sent to the listened account FROM the original Zalo app on another device now renders in zca-desktop in realtime as an outgoing bubble (previously never appeared). A send from this app still renders exactly once (same-device echo deduped), and self messages do not bump the unread badge. Non-secret outcome captured in the live-self-listen.txt sidecar; no thread ids, bodies, or credentials recorded.

knownRisks.description: Same-device echo dedupe matches on thread + body (text) or sticker id within a 15s window, not a stable server id, because Zalo redelivers the echo under a different msg_id. Two intentional identical-content sends to the same thread within 15s could collapse to one row/bubble.
knownRisks.acceptanceReason: Pre-existing design from fix-outgoing-echo-dupe; not introduced or worsened by enabling self_listen. Enabling self_listen is what makes the self-echo observable, but the dedupe that handles it was already in place and unit-tested. A future client-side correlation id would make reconciliation exact.
knownRisks.mitigation: If exactness is needed, add a per-send client correlation id and dedupe on it instead of content+window.
